steps.title: Identify the regrowth signal
steps.content: The passage states that environmental reforms and economic decline triggered “a natural regrowth process, as evident by the ______”. A regrowth process means forest cover returned and expanded after being cleared—so the completing evidence must show forest area increasing, not decreasing or remaining static.

steps.title: Match the signal to the data
steps.content: An increase in forest cover area for all classes from 1979 to 2000 (choice A) shows the recovery trend across the exact period following the mid-1980s reforms. The graph displays all three land classes rising from their 1979 low point through 2000, directly demonstrating the regrowth process. Choices B and C show comparisons or differences at single time points rather than a temporal trend of recovery; choice D shows the deforestation decline itself, not the regrowth that followed. That matches choice A — A is correct.
